body.title: [in_progress] Thread cwd through the domain layer so edit-capable runs isolate off _REPO_ROOT
body.description:
**Problem (CP6 safety gap, found firing the first harvest run):** AgenticLoop.run does `cwd = cwd or _REPO_ROOT` and the edit tools (_tool_bash shell=True, _tool_edit, _tool_write) execute against that cwd. ArchitectEditorFlow.run and AgenticLoop.run both ACCEPT cwd — but the DOMAIN layer (BaseDomain.run, BaseDomain._run_attempt, CodingDomain._run_attempt) threads NO cwd, so `resolve_domain('coding').run(ticket)` always falls back to _REPO_ROOT = the live UnseenUniversity repo. An edit-capable model (devstral/qwen-coder) run this way bashes/edits the live tree. A harvest run (and any direct domain.run) MUST be isolable to a throwaway dir.

**Proposed shape:** add an optional `cwd: Path | None = None` param threaded through the domain layer: BaseDomain.run(ticket, *, urgency, agent_id, cwd=None) -> BaseDomain._run_attempt(..., cwd=None) -> AgenticLoop.run(cwd=cwd); CodingDomain._run_attempt(..., cwd=None) -> ArchitectEditorFlow.run(cwd=cwd) and super()._run_attempt(cwd=cwd). Also thread CapabilityMixin.run_capability(ticket, *, agent_id, urgency, cwd=None) -> domain.run(cwd=cwd). Default None preserves today's _REPO_ROOT fallback (behavior-neutral for production DS, which relies on _REPO_ROOT being its own deployed tree).

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/domains/base.py (run + _run_attempt), unseen_university/devices/inference/domains/coding.py (_run_attempt), unseen_university/capabilities/base.py (run_capability pass-through).

**Design rules:** optional param default None = current behavior (no production change); log nothing new (cwd is an arg, not a state change); OOP-first preserved; no SQLite.

**Scope boundary:** thread the cwd param ONLY. NOT changing _REPO_ROOT default, NOT the harvest runner (separate), NOT any tool behavior.

**Validation:** BaseDomain.run(ticket, cwd=X) reaches _run_attempt with cwd=X (and thus the loop uses X, not _REPO_ROOT); cwd omitted -> _run_attempt gets None (unchanged).

**Observable via:** no new log line (arg-threading); verified by the loop's cwd resolving to the passed dir (edit tools write there). Test asserts the threaded value.

**Test plan:** hermetic - a recording BaseDomain subclass whose _run_attempt captures the cwd it received; assert run(ticket, cwd=tmp) threads tmp and run(ticket) threads None. PROOF NODE: run(ticket, cwd=tmp) -> _run_attempt sees cwd==tmp - red (run ignores cwd, _run_attempt sees None) -> green.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
